title: PermissionManagerServiceImpl updatePermissionFlagsForAllApps INVERTED ternary — SYSTEM_FIXED not modifiable on device but USER_SET is
notes: CODE BUG CONFIRMED in AOSP main, DEVICE PARTIAL. On Pixel 10 Android 16: (1) updatePermissionFlagsForAllApps callable without exception with SYSTEM_FIXED flag — but SYSTEM_FIXED flags do NOT actually change. Device code may differ from AOSP main or have additional protection. (2) HOWEVER: USER_SET flag IS modifiable via updatePermissionFlagsForAllApps — confirmed SET adds USER_SET to FOREGROUND_SERVICE_CAMERA (0x0→0x1), CLEAR removes USER_SET from POST_NOTIFICATIONS (0x305→0x304). This means the API is functional and affects ALL packages' ALL permissions. (3) Shell has GRANT_RUNTIME_PERMISSIONS, REVOKE_RUNTIME_PERMISSIONS, ADJUST_RUNTIME_PERMISSIONS_POLICY, INTERACT_ACROSS_USERS, INTERACT_ACROSS_USERS_FULL. (4) Cross-user modification (user 10) accepted without exception. (5) The inverted ternary is a real AOSP code bug but practical exploitation of SYSTEM_FIXED modification may be blocked on production devices. Downgraded to MEDIUM — still reportable as code-level bug.
